mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the positive direction over a 3-year rolling window, or forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero when FEDFUNDS > 2.0%.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the unemployment rate signals an imminent labor market contraction and reduced consumer spending, triggering risk-off positioning that drives negative S&P 500 forward returns within 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 65% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) when unemployment rises gradually below the 0.5pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, supporting corporate revenue growth and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST coincide with negative 60-day forward returns in > 55% of cases, or the interaction term yields a negative coefficient in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month acceleration in industrial production reflects immediate supply-side capacity utilization and operational efficiency, creating short-term positive momentum that pushes S&P 500 returns higher over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal reverses direction during supply-constrained inflationary periods, or 5-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.10) when INDPRO growth exceeds 1.0%.

mechanism_narrative: The co-occurrence of elevated consumer price inflation and restrictive policy rates creates a stagflationary environment that compresses profit margins and equity multiples, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in > 60% of instances where both thresholds are met, or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls indicates a resilient labor market that supports wage growth and aggregate demand, providing a fundamental floor for equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive payroll growth consistently coincides with negative 60-day forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 2.0% confirms macroeconomic expansion that exceeds baseline expectations, triggering institutional rebalancing into equities and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The threshold fails to predict direction, with positive 5-day returns occurring in < 50% of triggered cases, or the signal becomes noise when GDP revisions exceed 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between slowing industrial production and rising consumer prices indicates supply chain constraints that erode corporate margins, forcing equity deleveraging and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The negative signal is overridden by monetary easing expectations, resulting in positive forward returns in > 65% of cases, or the interaction term loses significance when energy prices stabilize.
